paper_title: A data-driven approach to snow reconstruction using tree rings

methods.method_name: Tree-Ring Data Compilation and Chronology Development
methods.description: This method involves compiling and processing tree-ring width measurements from various sites to develop standardized chronologies. These chronologies are used to analyze the influence of snowpack conditions on tree growth.

steps.description: We compiled raw total tree-ring width measurements from 954 sites for this study.
steps.searchable_summary: compile tree-ring width measurements from 954 sites

steps.description: All raw measurement series were power transformed to normalize age-related growth trends.
steps.searchable_summary: apply power transformation to normalize growth trends

steps.description: Detrended using a negative exponential curve or where that failed an age-dependent spline.
steps.searchable_summary: detrend data using negative exponential curve or age-dependent spline

steps.description: Standardized using a biweight robust mean to develop new versions of each tree-ring chronology for this study.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize data using biweight robust mean

steps.description: The expressed population signal (EPS) was used as an estimate of the adequacy of the sample size for capturing the hypothetical population growth signal.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate EPS to estimate sample size adequacy

steps.description: All chronologies were truncated at the conventional but arbitrary EPS value of 0.85.
steps.searchable_summary: truncate chronologies at EPS value of 0.85
